description:
REQ-030 Phase 5, items 12-14: The daemon handles outbound git operations (push, PR create) but has no inbound git event detection. Add polling-based detection of remote git state changes.

Changes needed:
1. Add `git_event_polling_enabled: bool` to DaemonProjectConfig (default: false)
2. Add `git_event_poll_interval_secs: Option<u64>` (default: 300 = 5min, separate from tick interval)
3. On each git event poll interval:
   a. Run `git fetch --quiet` to update remote refs
   b. Compare remote main HEAD with last-known SHA — detect new pushes
   c. Run `gh pr list --state open --json number,title,headRefName,updatedAt` — detect new/updated PRs
   d. Compare against last-known PR state (stored in .ao/state/git-event-state.json)
4. When new push detected: emit `git-push-detected` daemon event, optionally trigger configured pipeline
5. When new PR detected: emit `git-pr-opened` daemon event, optionally auto-create code-review task
6. Store git event state in `.ao/state/git-event-state.json` (last_remote_sha, known_pr_numbers)
7. All events flow through DaemonNotificationRuntime for webhook/Slack delivery
8. Respect active_hours gating
9. Add tests: push detection fires on new SHA, PR detection finds new PRs, deduplication prevents re-firing

Note: This overlaps with TASK-216 (trigger declarations for event-driven workflow dispatch from REQ-031). TASK-216 defines the trigger config schema; this task implements the actual git polling. They should be coordinated — this task provides the detection layer, TASK-216 provides the config-driven dispatch layer.

Key files:
- crates/orchestrator-core/src/daemon_config.rs
- crates/orchestrator-cli/src/services/runtime/runtime_daemon/daemon_git_helpers.rs
- crates/orchestrator-cli/src/services/runtime/runtime_daemon/daemon_scheduler_project_tick.rs
- New file: daemon_git_event_polling.rs
title: Add inbound git event polling for proactive workflow triggers
